text: implant. Once the device is in the desired location, roll the deployment wheel backward until a hard stop is felt and an audible click is heard. This will deploy the first implant. Roll the wheel forward until a hard stop is felt and an audible click is heard. This will confirm the first implant has been deployed. If there is resistance to moving the wheel during deployment, the needle may be against a bony surface. Try to move it slightly to free up space for the implant to deploy. Medial Graft Fixation Note: If using FiberStitch™ RC, simple, skip to page 13. Backward, first Forward, second 10 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 3 While keeping the tip of the device in the subacromial space, remove the delivery needle from the graft and move it to the desired location for the second implant, creating a mattress stitch. Advance the needle through the graft and cuff again. Once the device is in the desired location, roll the deployment wheel backward until a hard stop is felt and an audible click is heard. This will deploy the second implant. Roll the wheel forward until a hard stop is felt and an audible click is heard. This will confirm the second implant has been deployed. 4 Once both implants are deployed and the inserter is removed from the subacromial space, pinch all the sutures and gently pull to set the implants. 5 A suture loop and single suture will be visible outside the shoulder. Insert a probe or similar device through the loop and pull the loop of suture to reduce the suture spanning the two implants. 6 Tension the single suture to reduce the suture loop. The implants are secured when the loop is reduced, creating a mattress stitch configuration. CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 11 12 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 7 Load the tail of the suture into the loading wire and pull the tab to load the knot pusher. 8 Advance the knot pusher toward the graft, keeping it parallel to the suture while maintaining tension on the suture. Be careful not to cut the suture prematurely. Depress the plunger to cut the suture. Note: It is recommended to leave a few millimeters of suture tail before cutting. 9 Repeat steps 1-8 to complete medial fixation on the opposite corner of the graft. Alternative Option for Medial Fixation: FiberStitch™ RC Implant, Simple The FiberStitch RC implant, simple is available in both straight and curved options. This device creates a simple stitch, placing one implant underneath the rotator cuff and one implant on top of the graft. If using the FiberStitch RC implant, simple, complete steps 1-2 as shown above. After deployment of the first implant, retract the needle into the subacromial space, and deploy the second anchor by rolling the wheel back and forth a second time. Remove the inserter from the shoulder. Tension the single suture to reduce the second implant on top of the graft. A tension cutter can be used to provide counter tension. Complete steps 7-9 as shown above. CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 13 14 | CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System 1 While holding the graft spreader in place, uncleat and remove the #0 FiberWire ® sutures. Pull on one suture limb to remove it from the graft and graft spreader. Repeat on the other side. Note: It may be helpful to cut one limb of the FiberWire suture close to the PassPort Button™ cannula to reduce the friction on the suture during removal. 2 Uncleat the remaining lateral sutures from the graft spreader handle. Retract the arm by sliding the button backwards, and then carefully remove the graft spreader from the cannula. Graft Spreader Removal The CuffMend™ system can also be used over partial- thickness rotator cuff tears. Alternative option for Lateral Fixation: Knotless SwiveLock incorporation If desired, the knotless mechanism from a lateral row Knotless SwiveLock can be used for lateral fixation. If this technique is preferred, do not cinch the FiberLink/ TigerLink SutureTapes when prepping the graft. Use the links as a shuttle to bring the repair stitch through the graft. Convert through the knotless mechanism. For best results, it is important to make sure the graft is placed medially enough to not cover the lateral row anchors, and to make sure that the lateral row anchors are wider than the graft to prevent bunching. CuffMend™ Rotator Cuff Augmentation System | 15 Load one of the lateral SutureTapes through the eyelet of the 3.5 mm self-punching PushLock ® anchor. Taking care to avoid the existing lateral row anchors from the SpeedBridge™ repair construct, introduce the PushLock anchor through the lateral portal and insert the anchor.
